info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the \"Info and Links\".

q1.prompt: A traveler presenting with a localized "oriental sore" at the site of an insect bite is diagnosed with cutaneous leishmaniasis. Which of the following is the mandatory biological vector responsible for the transmission of the infective promastigotes to this patient?

q2.prompt: A patient with lymphatic filariasis is treated with doxycycline. Which of the following best explains the biological rationale for using this antibiotic to manage filarial nematode infections?

options.label: Direct inhibition of the adult worm's glucose uptake
Disruption of the helminthic microtubule polymerization
Induction of immediate paralysis in the microfilariae
Prevention of larval penetration into host lymphatics

q3.prompt: A 28-year-old woman develops violent upper abdominal pain and vomiting approximately four hours after consuming a meal of raw saltwater fish (sashimi). An upper endoscopy is performed to visualize the gastric mucosa. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

q4.prompt: A patient who previously recovered from a mild febrile illness caused by one serotype of Dengue virus is later infected with a different serotype. Which of the following clinical outcomes is this patient at significantly increased risk for developing due to the prior exposure?

options.label: Chronic asymptomatic carriage in the gallbladder
Development of a solitary pulmonary nodule
Severe plasma leakage and hemorrhagic fever

q5.prompt: A 7-year-old child is diagnosed with Hymenolepis nana infection. Which characteristic of this parasite's life cycle makes it unique among medically important cestodes?

options.label: It requires a large freshwater fish as a second intermediate host
It can complete its entire lifecycle within a single human host
It utilizes the pig as the mandatory definitive host for maturation
Its eggs must be ingested by a copepod to become infectious
It undergoes an obligate heart-lung migration phase in humans

q6.prompt: A laboratory technician is reviewing a blood smear from a patient who experiences cyclical fevers every 72 hours (quartan malaria). The technician identifies a schizont containing approximately eight merozoites arranged in a "rosette-like" configuration. Which species is most likely responsible?

q7.prompt: A patient presents with intense nocturnal pruritus and characteristic linear tracks (burrows) in the interdigital webs of the hands. Microscopic examination of skin scrapings confirms the presence of eight-legged mites. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial management?

options.label: Treatment with a single dose of praziquantel

q8.prompt: A researcher is mapping the geographic distribution of the sandfly vector to identify regions at risk for a specific parasitic disease. Which of the following clinical manifestations is the primary hallmark of the cutaneous form of this infection?

options.label: "Oriental sore" at the site of the bite

q9.prompt: Trichomonas vaginalis is characterized as an obligate parasite that exists only in the trophozoite stage. Which of the following best explains how this lack of a resistant cyst stage dictates its mode of transmission?

options.label: It must be transmitted via the bite of an arthropod vector
It requires a period of maturation in soil to become infectious
It can only be acquired through the ingestion of undercooked meat
It necessitates direct sexual contact for survival and spread
It relies on the ingestion of contaminated stagnant water
